title: Mid-Season Invitational | Play-Ins
content:
The competitive League of Legends ecosystem is buzzing following the latest announcement regarding 'Mid-Season Invitational | Play-Ins'. As the season progresses, this development has quickly taken center stage, drawing intense scrutiny from fans, analysts, and professional players alike. The core update highlights a significant shift that is expected to have wide-ranging consequences for the regional leagues and international tournaments.

In Europe's LEC, the atmosphere is electric as teams prepare to integrate these updates into their drafts. G2 Esports and Fnatic have historically led the charge in innovative pocket picks and creative map rotations. This new development could provide the perfect opportunity for rising rosters to challenge the established order and secure crucial championship points for the upcoming international events.

Speaking on the recent events, seasoned esports analysts noted that 'Mid-Season Invitational | Play-Ins  LoL Esports' is not just a temporary trend but a fundamental shift in how teams will approach their upcoming matchdays. 'Draft priority and map control are going to be completely redefined over the next few weeks,' remarked a prominent league caster. 'Teams that fail to recognize the gravity of this change will find themselves falling behind very quickly in the standings.'

As the community awaits the upcoming weekend matches, anticipation continues to build. Fans can catch all the high-stakes action live on official streaming channels, complete with expert desk analysis and live regional broadcasts. With so much on the line, the next chapter of the 2026 competitive season promises to deliver some of the most memorable moments in League of Legends esports history.

title: T1 continue strong start at Mid-Season Invitational
summary: T1 continue strong start at Mid-Season Invitational  Field Level Media
content:
T1 completed a 3-0 sweep of Karmine Corp on Monday to advance to Round 4 of the play-in stage at the League of Legends Mid-Season Invitational in Daejeon, South Korea.

T1 completed the Round 2 wins in 31 minutes, 35 minutes and 27 minutes. Karmine Corp dropped to Round 3 to face Team Liquid, with the winner meeting T1.

The play-in stage runs through Wednesday, with four teams competing in a double-elimination bracket. Teams are seeded according to results from the First Stand Tournament and are playing best-of-five matches. The eventual winner goes to the bracket stage, an eight-team tournament that provides a $2 million prize pool including a $500,000 check for the first-place finisher.

Soo-hwan “Peyz” Kim led T1 with 30 kills against Karmine Corp, which got 13 kills each from Chang-dong “Canna” Kim and Martin “Yike” Sundelin.

Team Liquid swept into Round 3 by eliminating Deep Cross Gaming, winning in 34, 33 and 25 minutes on Monday.

Brandon Joel “Josedeodo” Villegas and Hyeon-seung “Quid” Lim paced Team Liquid with 19 kills each. Three players had 10 kills in a losing effort for DCG.
